LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STEPHEN GUDE MOTION TO TAX COSTS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED, HOWEVER, ATTORNEY FEE AWARD SHALL BE PLAINTIFF'S AGREED REDUCED AMOUNT OF $12,806.38. BILLING RATE IS REASONABLE AND WORK PERFORMED REASONABLE AND NECESSARY. COSTS SUPPORTED BY HEIM DECLARATION AND TIME SHEETS SUBMITTED. THE COURT ADDS: INTEREST IS APPROPRIATE THE WAY IT WAS CALCULATED. (THE RULING IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT BRINGING OTHER MOTION IT FEELS MIGHT BE NECESSARY.) MOTION TO TAX COSTS
Off Calendar Jun-01-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT STEPHEN GUDE MOTION TO TAX COSTS. Appearances: Laura E. Perrault, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 722-6574 specially appearing for Defendant and Moving party; Jon S. Heim, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 898-1114 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied, however, attorney fee award shall be plaintiff's agreed reduced amount of $12,806.38. Billing rate is reasonable and work performed reasonable and necessary. Costs supported by Heim declaration and time sheets submitted. The Court adds: Interest is appropriate the way it was calculated. (The Ruling is without prejudice to defendant bringing other motion it feels might be necessary.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
